rationale: Novemsky and Kahneman (2005) is used in the citing paper's core theoretical positioning rather than as a peripheral literature-list mention. Weaver and Frederick explicitly contrast their reference-price account with pain-of-losing style explanations and invoke Novemsky and Kahneman when discussing boundary cases such as money and intended exchanges. They argue these cases are better explained by reference-price alignment, which reinterprets the prior account. This makes the citation function conceptually important to the paper's contribution.
relation_description: The citing paper challenges the cited paper's framing of boundary phenomena under loss-aversion/endowment explanations by offering an alternative mechanism. It argues that patterns associated with Novemsky and Kahneman (2005) are more parsimoniously explained by transaction disutility from reference-price comparisons.
